title: Cursor vs. incumbent AI coding assistant evaluation frame
subtitle: Use these criteria and pilot hypotheses in competitive deals. Avoid unsupported claims. Validate in the pilot.

criteria.name: Agentic workflows, not just suggestions
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Does Cursor complete multi-file tasks with a reviewable plan, not just inline completions?

criteria.name: Codebase context quality
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Does full-repo context reduce time spent searching and reading unrelated files?

criteria.name: Developer experience and adoption
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Will weekly active usage exceed 70% without a mandate?

criteria.name: Governance and enterprise controls
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Can admins enforce model policies, usage limits, and team structure at scale?

criteria.name: Security and privacy posture
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Does Privacy Mode and enterprise documentation satisfy InfoSec for approved repos?

criteria.name: Integration into existing workflows
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Does Cursor fit SSO, GitHub Enterprise, linters, and CI without breaking golden paths?

criteria.name: Pilot success metrics
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: Can we measure PR cycle time, hours saved, and satisfaction with agreed baselines?

criteria.name: Expansion potential
criteria.hypothesis: Hypothesis to validate: If the pilot team wins, can we replicate the playbook across adjacent engineering teams?
